hypothesis: The chemical template effect strength of a metal lattice, as quantified by the degree of electron localization at interstitial quasi-atom sites, will exhibit a systematic and transferable correlation with superconducting transition temperature across ternary metal superhydrides (M₁M₂Hₓ), such that ML models trained exclusively on binary superhydride metal lattice features can predict stable ternary superhydride compositions with Tc > 150 K at pressures below 200 GPa—a regime currently inaccessible to binary systems alone.
